title: trifolium fragiferum
intro: Trifolium fragiferum, also known as strawberry clover, is a perennial legume native to Europe, Asia, and Africa, characterized by its red, pink, or white flowers and distinctive strawberry-like appearance. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Trifolium fragiferum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support digestive function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
